text.title: Partnership Discovery and Scoring
text.summary: Partnership type taxonomy, candidate discovery sources, and a five-factor scorecard for evaluating fit before outreach.

section.title: Partnership Types
section.p: Taxonomy: Affiliate (partner promotes to their audience, you pay commission per sale), Co-marketing (shared audience access and content creation), Integration (technical connection benefiting both user bases), Reseller (partner sells and supports your product for a margin), White-label (partner distributes an unbranded version), Strategic (resource, credential, or market-access exchange).

rule.statement: For solopreneurs pre-scale: prioritize affiliates and co-marketing partnerships first. They require no technical integration and produce measurable results within 30–90 days.
rule.rationale: Integration and reseller partnerships require months of setup and have higher failure rates when one party lacks dedicated partnership headcount. Affiliates and co-marketing can be tested with a single piece of co-created content or a webinar.

section.title: Partner Discovery
section.p: Discovery sources: complementary products (same audience, different solution — signals: your customers mention them), influencers and content creators (bloggers, podcasters, YouTubers in your niche), adjacent communities (Slack groups, Discord, forums where your ICP gathers), adjacent businesses (same customer segment, non-competing offer).

rule.statement: Validate audience overlap before outreach: quote a specific data point ("their footer claims 12K customers") rather than asserting overlap. Agents and founders both overstate overlap from surface signals.
rule.rationale: Partnership proposals based on fabricated overlap are discovered immediately by the partner and destroy credibility before any relationship starts. Data-grounded claims signal research investment.

section.title: Partner Scorecard

rule.statement: Score every partner candidate on five factors (each 1–5): Reach, Relevance, Trust, Effort required (lower is better), Risk (lower is better). Total out of 25. Proceed only if score is 18 or above.
rule.rationale: Partnerships below 18/25 consistently fail because one of the five dimensions is weak enough to doom execution: low reach means no ROI, low trust means poor conversion, high effort means neither side prioritizes it.

section.reference: Scorecard calculator: ranks candidates 0–25, flags proceed/no
